# Feature: gate-hook

> Scope: scripts/gate.sh, hooks/hooks.json
> Stack: shell/bash, python3 (json parsing only)

## What it does

Pre-write hook that protects invariant files (`specs/_invariants/i_*`) from direct modification. When Claude Code attempts to Write or Edit an invariant file, gate.sh blocks the operation (exit code 2) unless a bypass lock file exists with a matching target. This enforces the invariant read-only gate — the only way to update invariant files is through `purlin:invariant sync`, which creates a temporary bypass lock.

## Rules

- RULE-1: Writes to paths matching `specs/_invariants/i_*` are blocked with exit code 2
- RULE-2: Writes to non-invariant paths always pass (exit code 0)
- RULE-3: When `.purlin/runtime/invariant_write_lock` exists and its `target` field basename matches the file being written, the write is allowed (exit code 0)
- RULE-4: When the lock file exists but targets a different file, the write is still blocked (exit code 2)
- RULE-5: The blocked error message is written to stderr (not stdout) and tells the user to run `purlin:invariant sync`
- RULE-6: The hook is registered in hooks.json as a PreToolUse hook matching `Write|Edit|NotebookEdit`

## Proof

- PROOF-1 (RULE-1): Attempt a write to `specs/_invariants/i_test.md` with no lock; verify exit code 2
- PROOF-2 (RULE-2): Attempt a write to `specs/mcp/foo.md`; verify exit code 0
- PROOF-3 (RULE-3): Create a lock file targeting `i_test.md`, then attempt a write to that file; verify exit code 0
- PROOF-4 (RULE-4): Create a lock file targeting `i_other.md`, then attempt a write to `i_test.md`; verify exit code 2
- PROOF-5 (RULE-5): Capture stderr from a blocked write; verify it contains "purlin:invariant sync"
- PROOF-6 (RULE-6): Parse hooks.json; verify PreToolUse entry matches `Write|Edit|NotebookEdit` and runs gate.sh

## Implementation Notes

- Design pattern: path matching via bash `case` statement handles both absolute and relative paths (scripts/gate.sh:9-12)
- Lock validation: uses python3 one-liner to parse JSON lock file, not jq (scripts/gate.sh:17)
- Basename comparison: compares basenames only to handle path format variance (scripts/gate.sh:19-21)
